meta.title: ADGM Registration Authority Welcome
speakers: His Excellency Rashed Abdulkarim Al Blooshi

transcript:
Bismillah al-Rahman al-Rahim. Sheikh Zayed bin Suroor bin Mohammed Al Nahyan, Your Excellencies, Ladies and Gentlemen. It is my honor today to welcome you to the Solana Breakpoint 2025 and welcoming you to Abu Dhabi. To us, this is a significant moment for the first time that we see Solana 2025, the Breakpoint, comes to the Middle East.

The vision of the UAE always around the digital age. And we always think of the blockchain and tokenization that are reshaping the global commerce and why UAE in general is trying to be part of this transformation. We at ADGM, our role is to bring the national vision of life and an operational reality. To provide the regulatory infrastructure where innovation meets institutional confidence. Where protocol can be built, scaled, and serve the global market with the confidence.

If we look around us in Abu Dhabi this week, we are in the final stages of Abu Dhabi Finance Week. We are at the final stages of the 300 conferences with 800 speakers and 31,000 attending that conference. Out of those 31,000s, we have 5,000 companies. The assets that are managed by those companies worth more than 60 trillion dollars. That is more than 50% of the world GDP.

At the same time, we are about to finalize the Bitcoin MENA which is related to the digital assets community. The Formula F1, while I was coming here, I still hear the noise of those cars and we still see the vibration of the cars around us. And that brought attention to Abu Dhabi. Now, we are enjoying our moments with all of you at the Breakpoint and we see thousands of builders and innovators around the Solana ecosystem, all presents and available here in Abu Dhabi.

This is not a coincidence. This is convergence. Traditional finance, digital assets, global capital, all at the same time in the same place. Abu Dhabi is the Capital of Capital. And increasingly it is also becoming the Capital of the Blockchain Innovation.

At ADGM, we do not make promises about the framework we intend to build. We point to what we have already delivered. In 2018, ADGM introduced the world's first comprehensive regulatory framework for the virtual assets. No pilot, no sandbox. A complete operational regime covering the full spectrum of the digital assets activity.

In 2023, we launched the DLT Foundations Regulations, the world's first purpose-built legal framework to blockchain protocols, Decentralized Autonomous Organizations, and emerging Web3 structure. For the first time, decentralized entities could achieve legal recognition while preventing the governance decentralized valuation.

Today, ADGM provide regulatory clarity across the full range of blockchain activities: custody, trading, lending, tokenization and real-world assets, and utility token issuance. And we continue to evolve. This year we announce strategic partnership to advance cross-chain compliance infrastructure. We have published guidance on staking activities. We are actively engaging with the convergence of the AI and blockchain.

In addition to that, to ensure ADGM remains at the frontier of technologies valuation, this year we have established a dedicated emerging technologies function in ADGM. The team focuses on blockchain, AI, crypto mining, and utility token issuance and support protocol.

I encourage all of us around this area, whether you are a protocol considering international expansion, or you are a developer seeking regulatory clarity, or you are an institutional exploring digital assets integration, to connect, communicate, and take advantage of us being around you here.

To the Solana Foundation, thank you for bringing Breakpoint to Abu Dhabi. This is partnership reflects a shared ambitious to build infrastructure that connects blockchain innovation with the institution finance and do the regulatory maturity that sustainable the growth of the economies. I really encourage all of us to take advantage of such a reputed events and wish us all a best luck. Thank you very much.

[![Tether.io](https://tether.io/wp-content/themes/tether-io-theme/assets/images/logo.svg)](https://tether.io)

![](https://tether.io/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/ADGM.png)
[Others](https://tether.io/news?category=others)
December 8, 2025
![Print](https://tether.io/wp-content/themes/tether-io-theme/assets/images/print.svg)
Tether’s USD₮ Recognised as Accepted Fiat-Referenced Token in Abu Dhabi’s ADGM for Use on Several Major Blockchains\*
**8 December 2025**: –[Tether](http://tether.io), the largest company in the digital assets industry, announced today, that USD₮ issued on a number of major blockchains has been[recognised as an](https://assets.adgm.com/download/assets/AFRT+List+November+2025.pdf/c1b34426b88e11f0912ade26907f67ec)[Accepted Fiat-Referenced Token](https://assets.adgm.com/download/assets/AFRT+List+November+2025.pdf/c1b34426b88e11f0912ade26907f67ec)(AFRT) within ADGM. The recognition allows Authorised Persons licensed by the Financial Services Regulatory Authority (FSRA) of ADGM to offer Regulated Activities involving USD₮ on Aptos, Celo, Cosmos, Kaia, Near, Polkadot, Tezos, TON, and TRON.
This milestone follows continued close engagement with the FSRA to demonstrate the resilience, transparency, and compliance-focused approach of Tether’s operations. This approval brings expanded access for ADGM Authorised Persons to the world’s most widely used stablecoin across multiple blockchain networks.
“The UAE continues to set the global standard for digital asset regulation, and Tether is proud to contribute to this leadership,” said**Paolo Ardoino, CEO of Tether**. “This milestone highlights Tether’s dedication to advancing financial inclusion and innovation on a global scale. Introducing USD₮ within ADGM’s regulated digital asset framework reinforces the role of stablecoins as essential components of today’s financial landscape. It also creates fresh opportunities for collaboration and growth throughout the Middle East. By extending recognition to USD₮ on several major blockchains, ADGM further strengthens Abu Dhabi’s position as a global hub for compliant digital finance.”
The approval builds upon[ADGM’s previous recognition of USD₮ on Ethereum, Solana, and Avalanche](https://tether.io/news/tethers-usdt-recognized-as-accepted-virtual-asset-in-adgm/), and forms part of a broader wave of new network approvals that extends USD₮’s regulatory coverage far beyond the original three chains. Together, these approvals reinforce ADGM’s forward-thinking approach, creating a comprehensive multi-chain foundation for USD₮ within one of the world’s most respected digital-asset jurisdictions. This allows ADGM Authorised Persons to support a broader range of blockchain networks.
With this decision, Tether now has USD₮ recognized on nearly all the major blockchains it supports, including Aptos, Celo, Cosmos, Kaia, Near, Polkadot, Tezos, TON and TRON.
This multi-chain approval enhances interoperability across the global financial system, allowing USD₮ to function as a reliable settlement asset for trading and decentralized applications, while maintaining the AFRT criteria and safeguards required by the FSRA. It also demonstrates how collaboration between innovators and regulators can expand financial access without compromisin

# Ripple’s RLUSD recognized as Accepted Fiat-Referenced Token for use within Abu Dhabi’s ADGM
Insight Author Avatar
Team Ripple
Nov 27, 2025
*Approval from FSRA enables use within ADGM, positioning RLUSD for further expansion across the Middle East*
**Abu Dhabi, UAE —November 27, 2025 –**[Ripple](https://ripple.com/home/), a financial technology company that offers crypto solutions for businesses, today announced that its USD-backed stablecoin Ripple USD (RLUSD) has been recognized as an Accepted Fiat-Referenced Token by Abu Dhabi’s Financial Services Regulatory Authority (FSRA), enabling use within the ADGM, the international financial centre of Abu Dhabi, the Capital of the United Arab Emirates (UAE).
RLUSD is now eligible for use by Authorised Persons licensed by the FSRA to carry out applicable Regulated Activities, provided that they satisfy and maintain compliance with firm-level regulatory obligations relevant to the use of Fiat-Referenced Tokens. Noting ADGM’s standing as a leading international financial centre known for its robust and progressive regulatory standards, this represents a further significant step in establishing Ripple’s stablecoin as a trusted, compliant settlement asset in the Middle East. In addition to the FSRA’s recognition, RLUSD also recently[received approval](https://ripple.com/ripple-press/rlusd-stablecoin-dfsa-approval/)from other regulators within the region.
“The FSRA’s recognition of RLUSD as a Fiat-Referenced Token reinforces our commitment to regulatory compliance and trust - two non-negotiables when it comes to institutional finance,” said**Jack McDonald, Senior Vice President of Stablecoins at Ripple**. “With a market capitalization of over $1 billion and growing adoption in core financial uses like collateral and payments, RLUSD is quickly becoming a go-to USD stablecoin for major institutions. This momentum is helping drive the next wave of secure, compliant digital asset adoption around the world.”
Issued under a New York Department of Financial Services (NYDFS) Limited Purpose Trust Company Charter, RLUSD has reached a market capitalization of over $1.2 billion since its launch in late 2024. With stringent safeguards including 1:1 USD backing by high-quality liquid assets, strict reserve management and asset segregation, third-party attestation and clear redemption rights, RLUSD is designed to meet the highest expectations of both regulators and institutional users.
“The UAE continues to set a global benchmark for digital asset regulation and innovation,” said**Reece Merrick, Managing Director, Middle East and Africa at Ripple**. “ADGM is recognized globally for its robust and forward-thinking regulatory leadership, so this approval reinforces RLUSD as a compliant stablecoin that meets the highest standards of trust, transparency, and utility. This recognition is yet another step forward for Ripple’s operations in the region, where we are experiencing surging interest in our products. We look forward to continuing working with our partners and regulators to support the safe and rapid adoption of digital asset technology across the Middle East.”
“We congratulate Ripple on achieving this important milestone,” said**Arvind Ramamurthy, Chief Market Development Officer, ADGM**. “We look forward to seeing them make use of our robust regulatory framework, designed to support the sustainable growth of innovative firms and ensure the highest international standards of governa

tags.impact_reason: ADGM's welcoming of Solana Breakpoint 2025 and recognition of digital assets within its regulatory framework signals growing institutional acceptance and potential for increased adoption of blockchain technologies in the region.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a monumental, architectural data-vault as the anchor because it fuses the institutional gravitas of ADGM (represented by marble and grand architecture) with the high-tech nature of blockchain 'rails' (represented by server racks and fiber optics). This visually represents the core theme of convergence between traditional capital and emerging technology in Abu Dhabi. I included the text '$60T' as a vast holographic projection to emphasize the immense scale of capital mentioned in the story, making it a diegetic part of the futuristic, high-stakes environment.
visual_idea.description: A colossal structure, evoking the clean, geometric lines of modern Abu Dhabi architecture, stands in a cavernous, dark space. The walls are a seamless fusion of polished white marble and racks of glowing server blades. Thick, pulsing fiber optic cables, acting as 'on-chain rails,' run along the structure's contours. In the vast air beside the main tower, a monumental holographic projection of the text '$60T' glows with a soft, blue light, casting volumetric shafts through the ambient haze. The background recedes into darkness, hinting at an endless digital treasury.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A colossal structure, evoking the clean, geometric lines of modern Abu Dhabi architecture, stands in a cavernous, dark space. The walls are a seamless fusion of polished white marble and racks of glowing server blades. Thick, pulsing fiber optic cables, acting as 'on-chain rails,' run along the structure's contours. In the vast air beside the main tower, a monumental holographic projection of the text '$60T' glows with a soft, blue light, casting volumetric shafts through the ambient haze. The background recedes into darkness, hinting at an endless digital treasury. Emphasize epic scale and immense height, with ample negative space at the top to convey monumental presence. The scene is illuminated by cinematic volumetric lighting, creating dramatic contrast and ethereal light shafts through the ambient haze. The polished white marble surfaces reflect light with exquisite detail, and the luminous fiber optics emit a vibrant glow. 3:4 aspect ratio, 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, ultra-photorealistic, high dynamic range, architectural photography.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a monumental, architectural data-vault as the anchor because it fuses the institutional gravitas of ADGM (represented by marble and grand architecture) with the high-tech nature of blockchain 'rails' (represented by server racks and fiber optics). This visually represents the core theme of convergence between traditional capital and emerging technology in Abu Dhabi. I included the text '$60T' as a vast holographic projection to emphasize the immense scale of capital mentioned in the story, making it a diegetic part of the futuristic, high-stakes environment.
visual_idea.description: A macro, abstract shot focusing on the precise intersection of materials. A thick bundle of luminous, gold fiber optic cables is embedded flawlessly into a slab of cool, white veined marble. The light from the cables pulses gently, creating a soft glow that highlights the perfect, clean edge where the organic stone meets the man-made technology. The background is a deep, out-of-focus bokeh of distant blue server lights, emphasizing the fusion of tradition and innovation.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A captivating macro, abstract shot, perfectly symmetrical and centrally focused, filling the 1:1 square frame with exquisite texture. The precise intersection of materials is highlighted: a thick, lustrous bundle of luminous, glowing gold fiber optic cables is flawlessly embedded into a highly polished slab of cool, white veined marble. The marble exhibits intricate natural patterns and a mirror-like finish, beautifully reflecting the ambient light. The light from the fiber optic cables pulses gently, casting a soft, ethereal glow that dramatically emphasizes the immaculate, clean edge where the organic stone seamlessly meets the advanced, man-made technology. The scene is illuminated by cinematic volumetric lighting, creating a high dynamic range with dramatic, soft ambient glow and subtle reflections. In the deep background, an out-of-focus bokeh of distant, subtle blue server lights creates a sense of vast architectural data-vaults, underscoring a profound fusion of tradition and innovation. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, ultra-photorealistic, cinematic.

editorial.headline: ADGM Unveils Emerging Tech Division; $60T Capital Pool Eyes On-Chain Rails
editorial.standfirst: Abu Dhabi Global Market establishes a dedicated unit for AI and blockchain convergence while finalizing staking rules, signaling the jurisdiction is now fully operational for institutional capital deployment.
key_takeaways: New Division: ADGM launches dedicated 'Emerging Tech' function to regulate AI, crypto mining, and utility tokens.
Capital Depth: Abu Dhabi Finance Week 2025 hosted firms representing $60 Trillion AUM—over 50% of global GDP.
Regulatory Alpha: Tether and Circle secure major approvals in Dec 2025; new staking framework proposed.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
The convergence of traditional finance and decentralized protocols has moved beyond theoretical pilots. Speaking at **Solana Breakpoint 2025**, His Excellency Rashed Abdulkarim Al Blooshi confirmed that **Abu Dhabi Global Market (ADGM)** has transitioned from a regulatory sandbox to a full-scale operational regime capable of handling the world’s deepest capital pools. With **Abu Dhabi Finance Week** convening firms representing **$60 trillion in AUM**—more than half of global GDP—the jurisdiction is aggressively positioning itself as the primary liquidity bridge for institutional crypto.

For investors seeking alpha, the most significant development is the establishment of ADGM’s dedicated **Emerging Technologies** function. Moving beyond simple asset custody, this new division is explicitly tasked with regulating the complex intersection of **Artificial Intelligence (AI)** and blockchain, as well as crypto mining and utility token issuance. This forward-looking structure addresses the next cycle’s critical narrative: the financialization of compute and the governance of decentralized autonomous organizations (DAOs), which received their own purpose-built legal framework in 2023.

ADGM’s "no pilot, no sandbox" philosophy is yielding tangible institutional adoption. In December 2025 alone, the jurisdiction saw **Tether (USDT)** recognized as an Accepted Fiat-Referenced Token across multiple chains—including **Solana**—and **Circle** securing full licensing. Furthermore, the regulator has advanced a strategic partnership with **Hacken** to build cross-chain compliance infrastructure and published new guidance on staking activities, providing the clarity required for large-scale institutional participation in network security.

This regulatory maturity is accelerating the migration of top-tier entities to the UAE. With **Binance** securing full approval and **Galaxy Digital** establishing operations in the capital, Abu Dhabi is effectively engineering a "Capital Network" where digital assets and traditional finance interact under English common law. For developers and VCs at Breakpoint, the message is clear: the infrastructure to deploy billions on-chain is not just coming; it is already live.
